dependencies: Reliance on suppliers and commodity markets for components such as motors, valves, copper, aluminum, steel, semi-conductors and electronic components
Certain proprietary component parts provided by single-source unaffiliated third-party suppliers
Some key parts available only from a single supplier or limited group of suppliers
risks: Supply chain disruptions from extreme weather and climate events
Supplier capacity constraints or production disruptions
Price increases, duties, tariffs or other government actions
Shortages of raw materials and components
Work stoppages and labor relations issues at suppliers
Regulatory changes impacting suppliers including climate-related regulations

regulatory_risks: Paris Agreement and U.S. climate policies to meet nationally determined contributions
Local regulations reducing use of fossil fuels in buildings
Kigali Amendment to the Montreal Protocol requiring curtailment of high global warming potential refrigerants
American Innovation and Manufacturing (AIM) Act of 2020 regulating refrigerants
Building energy and cold chain efficiency requirements
Natural gas prohibitions
Public disclosure rules surrounding greenhouse gas emissions (California and European Union)
Changes in tax laws and regulations
Import/export regulations and trade restrictions
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) compliance
Antitrust and anti-collusion laws
Anti-money laundering regulations
Export Administration Regulations administered by U.S. Department of Commerce
Embargoes and sanctions regulations administered by U.S. Department of the Treasury
Environmental regulations on refrigerants, noise levels, product and fire safety, hydrofluorocarbon emissions, fluorinated gases, hazardous substances and electronic equipment waste
Product safety standards and certifications
Data privacy and cybersecurity laws
U.S. government contracting regulations
FDA approval requirements
Asbestos-related regulations
PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances) regulations related to Aqueous Film Forming Foam (AFFF)
Global chemical use restrictions for human health and environmental protection
OECD/G20 two-pillar global tax reform plan (Pillar One and Pillar Two)
GILTI (global intangible low-taxed income) tax
Fragmented country or regional specific regulatory requirements

sanction_risks: Russia sanctions - suspended business operations in Russia in March 2022
Compliance with sanctions and trade controls
Potential new tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico and China announced in February 2025
U.S. trade policy uncertainty including changes in tariffs, trade agreements and trade restrictions
